name: Hong Kong Stock Exchange
shortDescription: Asia's largest biotech IPO venue after Chapter 18A (2018) opened the main board to pre-revenue biotechs.
narrativeHook: On 30 April 2018 HKEX rewrote the listing map for unprofitable Chinese biotech: Chapter 18A let pre-revenue companies list with one core product past the concept stage and HK$1.5B expected market cap, and the wave that followed peaked at twenty 18A IPOs in 2021.

exchangeContext.keyRule: Main Board Listing Rules Chapter 18A (2018) for pre-revenue biotechs; Chapter 18C (2023) for specialist tech (incl. AI biotech)
exchangeContext.hookFact: The 18A wave from 2018–2021 absorbed roughly 95% of the global pre-revenue biotech IPO volume that didn't list on Nasdaq, and remained the primary listing route for Chinese biotech even through the post-2022 financing slump.

timeline.status: Chapter 18A Main Board listing rules added 30 April 2018; pre-revenue biotechs eligible.
timeline.note: BeiGene, Innovent, Junshi, Hua Medicine were the first 18A vintage; 7 listed in 2018, 9 in 2019.

timeline.status: 18A pace continued; Akeso, RemeGen, JW Therapeutics among the 2020 cohort.
Peak 20 18A listings in 2021; pace fell to 8 in 2022 and 7 in 2023 as financing tightened.
timeline.note: STAR Market opened a complementary domestic route from 2019.

timeline.status: Chapter 18C added 31 March 2023 to host pre-revenue specialist-tech (incl. AI biotech) listings.
XtalPi (Jun 2024) became the first Chapter 18C listing; Duality, Insilico continued the 18A pipeline through 2025.

name: STAR Market
shortDescription: Shanghai Stock Exchange's Sci-Tech Innovation Board; the domestic counterpart to HKEX 18A for pre-revenue tech and biotech.
narrativeHook: Launched on 22 July 2019 with a registration-based IPO regime and a fifth listing standard explicitly for pre-revenue biotechs, STAR became the first onshore market in China to allow loss-making biotech to list — closing the financing loop that HKEX 18A had opened a year earlier.

exchangeContext.keyRule: Set 5 of the STAR listing standards: pre-revenue biotech with at least one core product past Phase 2
exchangeContext.hookFact: STAR introduced a registration-based system in China for the first time, weighting issuer disclosure over administrative pre-approval — a structural break from the historical IPO queue.

timeline.status: Announced by President Xi at the 2018 China International Import Expo; SSE began rule drafting late 2018.
First 25 STAR listings on 22 July 2019; biotech standard 5 (pre-revenue) used by Suzhou Zelgen, Shanghai Junshi (existing HKEX-listed firms with A-share dual listings).
STAR biotech listings included BeiGene's secondary 2021 listing (688235) — first biotech with NASDAQ + HKEX + STAR triple listing.
Slowdown in 2023–2024 as broader A-share IPO pace cooled; STAR retained the domestic biotech IPO mantle.
